President Taft's visit to Spokane.
September q, are working hard
and holding meetings daily. A COM
plete report Is expected to be made
to the general committee by next
Tuesday.


An invitation has been received
by the chamber of commerce from
the commercial club of Chicago to
attend the conference Of the lead
ing commercial bodies of the coun-
try to be held at Chicago Septem-
Der II and IS.


An oriental lawn party, decorated
with Japanese lanterns and having
other features suggestive of the
land of the mikado, will be one of
the features of the entertainment
of the Japanese commercial visit
ors, who are expected to arrive in
Spokane September II. f. e. Good
all is chairman of the committee in
charge of the reception.


The school board, after an all day
session, selected Richard T. Har-
greaves as principal of the North
Central high school to succeed the
late Professor Beare. Mr.. Har-
greaces was the history teacher at
the school and during the absence
from the work Of Professor Beare
acted as principal. He Is "a years
old, married, and came here from
Topeka, Kan, over year ago.
There were 20 applicants for the
place.


The case Of the North Coast In
rebuttal was being heard today in
its suit in the federal court to con-
denim portion of the Jenkins tract
belonging to the Spokane, Portland
&Seattle. The testimony of the
Hill forces was concluded yester.
day.


Rev. John w. Berger, religious
director for the Y. M. c. A. for
over year, has handed In his
resignation, to take effect Septem
Der 15. Mr.. Berger will go to San
Francisco to take charge of the
Peoples Place, a nonsectarian in.
stitution having charge of settle.
ment and church work.
